task_text: Open project_helios_status.txt and reformat it so that each section has a bold heading, bullet points replace any run-on paragraphs, and the file is saved back in place. Do not change any of the factual content.

content.text: Sure! Let me start by reading the file so I can see its current content.

content.content:
**Project Helios — Weekly Status Update**
- Week of 2026-07-06

**Overall**
- ON TRACK (minor risks)

**Milestone Progress**
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

**Sprint 14 Goals**
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

**Open Blockers**
- 1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
  - Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
- 2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
  - Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

**Next Milestone**
- Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

content.text:
The file is already well-formatted — it has bold headings, bullet points throughout, and no run-on paragraphs. Every section already meets the formatting criteria you described:

- **Bold headings** ✅ — All section titles use `**...**` markdown bold.
- **Bullet points** ✅ — All content is already presented as bullet points, including sub-bullets for blockers.
- **No run-on paragraphs** ✅ — There is no prose that needs to be broken up.

No changes are needed, and overwriting the file with identical content would be a no-op. The file is already in the correct format — all factual content is intact and untouched. Let me know if you'd like any other adjustments!
